channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Orthodox Jewish women in Israel may now take a rabbinic exam, like men
item.description: Until now, only Orthodox Jewish men in Israel have been allowed to take exams to become rabbis. After a long campaign, women can now also take the test - but still can't become rabbis
item.content:encoded:
Until now, only Orthodox Jewish men in Israel have been allowed to take exams to become rabbis. After a long campaign, women can now also take the test - but still can't become rabbis

(Image credit: Ofir Berman for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Daniel Estrin

item.title: This is how close American households are to the financial edge
item.description: A new report shows that nearly half of U.S. households did not earn enough to cover their necessities in 2024.
item.content:encoded:
A new report shows that nearly half of U.S. households did not earn enough to cover their necessities in 2024.

(Image credit: Brandon Bell)
item.dc:creator: Stephan Bisaha

item.title: U.S. strikes Iran again. And, DOJ charges Google staffer over Polymarket trades
item.description: The U.S. says it has struck Iran again as peace talks continue to end the conflict. And, the federal government has charged a Google staffer for allegedly using insider information in Polymarket trades.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. says it has struck Iran again as peace talks continue to end the conflict. And, the federal government has charged a Google staffer for allegedly using insider information in Polymarket trades.

(Image credit: Win McNamee)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: A federal judge in D.C. declines to block Trump's executive order on voting by mail
item.description: A federal judge in Washington, D.C., has declined to temporarily block President Trump's executive order that calls for restricting mail-in voting. Another judge may rule on the order soon.
item.content:encoded:
A federal judge in Washington, D.C., has declined to temporarily block President Trump's executive order that calls for restricting mail-in voting. Another judge may rule on the order soon.

(Image credit: Alex Brandon)
item.dc:creator: Hansi Lo Wang

item.title: U.S. street drug deaths keep dropping, but some Western states see deadly overdose surge
item.description: Researchers say some structural changes in treating addiction are helping, including wider access to overdose reversing medication.
item.content:encoded:
Researchers say some structural changes in treating addiction are helping, including wider access to overdose reversing medication.

(Image credit: John Raby/AP)
item.dc:creator: Brian Mann

item.title: An Ohio pastor-turned-lawmaker backs a Charlie Kirk American Heritage Act for schools
item.description: The bill says it would permit the teaching of the positive impact of Judeo-Christian values in U.S. history. Opponents say it offers a skewed view of history.
item.content:encoded:
The bill says it would permit the teaching of the positive impact of Judeo-Christian values in U.S. history. Opponents say it offers a skewed view of history.

(Image credit: Andrew Spear for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Sarah Donaldson

item.title: Trump pledged to house 6,000 homeless vets. His budget funds zero
item.description: Veteran groups hoped a Trump executive order would supercharge housing for homeless vets. That hasn't happened, and veterans' advocates want to know why.
item.content:encoded:
Veteran groups hoped a Trump executive order would supercharge housing for homeless vets. That hasn't happened, and veterans' advocates want to know why.

(Image credit: Bethany Mollenkof for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Quil Lawrence

item.title: Trick or spice? How the 'nutmeg' came to dominate the soccer world
item.description: Nutmeg commonly spices up a holiday season eggnog. It's also one of the most sought-after trick moves in soccer.
item.content:encoded:
Nutmeg commonly spices up a holiday season eggnog. It's also one of the most sought-after trick moves in soccer.

(Image credit: Sarah Stier)
item.dc:creator: Ava Berger

item.title: These swing voters are adding high gas prices into their political calculations
item.description: As part of NPR's Swing Shift project checking in with swing voters, these Americans are split on who is to blame for high gas prices but they all agree it has an impact on their personal finances.
item.content:encoded:
As part of NPR's Swing Shift project checking in with swing voters, these Americans are split on who is to blame for high gas prices but they all agree it has an impact on their personal finances.

(Image credit: Illustrations by Tara Anand)
item.dc:creator: Tamara Keith

item.title: Trump wants a gas tax holiday. There's a much bigger problem looming
item.description: Suspending the federal gasoline tax could save drivers up to 18.4 cents per gallon. But it would drain the fund meant to cover roadbuilding and repairs — a fund that's already in trouble.
item.content:encoded:
Suspending the federal gasoline tax could save drivers up to 18.4 cents per gallon. But it would drain the fund meant to cover roadbuilding and repairs — a fund that's already in trouble.

(Image credit: Mario Tama)
item.dc:creator: Camila Domonoske
